steps.title: Find the three-unit growth
steps.content:
The ratio from $x=3$ to $x=6$ is
$$
\frac{f(6)}{f(3)}=\frac{216}{8}=27
$$
This is the multiplier for a $3$-unit increase in $x$.

steps.title: Find the one-unit multiplier
steps.content: Since $27=3^3$, the multiplier for each $1$-unit increase in $x$ is $3$.

steps.title: Move from six to eight
steps.content: From $x=6$ to $x=8$ is a $2$-unit increase, so multiply by $3^2$: $$ f(8)=216(3^2)=216(9)=1{,}944 $$ The answer is 1944.
